core_content.markdown:
# Amid all the talk about President Musk, is Donald already tiring of his First Buddy?
Grabbing the limelight: Elon Musk and Donald Trump watch a SpaceX Starship launch
OUNDING on to the stage and jumping in the air with arms outstretched,Elon Musklookedlike aman ho'd just won the lottery.DonaldTrump had asked him to join m on stage for the first time at an eleconrally inButler,Pennsylvania,last ctober-the town where, just a few onths earlier, the ex-president had He is a truly incredible guy -and I don't aythat thatoften,'Trump declaredofthe coon who'd sunkno lessthan £2lmillion to his second presidential campaign. merica Great Again],I'm dark Maga,'Iusk told the cheering crowd as he ointed to his Trump cap-not the usual d.but black. itics might say that never a truer word as spoken in jest as Musk has behaved ren more pugnaciously and wildly than rump himself. oliticiansaroundthe globe-fromRepublians inWashington over governmentspendgto European administrations,notably eir Starmer overthe grooming gang scandal northern Englandandalso Germanyover omigration andcrime. vorn in,Musk has effectively hijackedhis esidency,soaking up the world's attenon with his endless outbursts on X,his cial media platform on which he has lmillionfollowers. reets throughthenight while runninga ft of globalbusinesses,already overayed his hand-risking killing his broance'with the president-elect before the cond term hasevenstarted? ised by Trump biographer and New York mes reporterMaggie Haberman. ep connections in the court of The Donentoldthat Trump is already ring of the company of the orld'srichestman. And Trump es complain a bit to people about how Musk is around a lot. Musk is around a lot'primarily because, since the election in November,he has rented a cottage'-which usually costs at least £1,600 a night-on the groundsof Trump's main home, his private Mar-a-Lago club in Palm Beach,Florida.Consequently,heis only a couple of hundred feet from the mansion in which Trump is forming his next administration. Musk has been able to drop in to meetings and dinners with the president-elect, such as one he recently held with Amazon tycoon Jeff Bezos.and join his telephone calls. into the Mar-a-Lago compound,Musk had parked himself in Trump's face'. crats about 'President Musk'and Trump's First Lady being named Elonia’are increasingly riling the 78-year-old. RUMP insiders have said much the same to news website Mediaite,claiming the president-elect was tiring of Musk's omnipresence'and the attention he was drawing. 100 per cent,Trump is annoyed said a source who worked on the 2024 election campaign.'There's a Chinese saying:"Two tigers cannot live on one mountaintop".' Another insider was quoted as saying:Someone that is around Giiamucmandmavigmuence would be a bother...I mean,the guy came in, gave a boatload of money and wants to take over made a fair few enemies in the Trump camp with his reportedly aggressive behaviour towards them,so there may be a strong element of wishful thinking that he's on his way out. For the moment,there's no public sign of a rift. Trump has asked Musk to co-lead (with fellowconservative entrepreneur Vivek Ramaswamy)a new'Department of Government Efficiency',or Doge, although sceptics point out that only Congress can create new federal agencies and that this department is unlikely to get off the ground. fect excuse to avoid his'First Buddy'-as the tycoon calls himself-although Musk could always ask for something else.After all,whileopponentscomplainnes never been elected to public office post now),nor hadTrump's son-(and so shouldn't be given a big in-law Jared Kushner when the ambitious young property developer was put in charge of leading US efforts to bring peace to the Middle East during his first term. want to stay as physically close to Trump as possible -which, from January 20,means an office on the White House campus. But at the same time, she ally slow to dispense with people acknowledges Trump is tradition-who have been useful to him and Haberman doesn't believe Musk cannot afford to antagonise Musk.That's not only because of his vast wealth-and willingness to plough it into polities-or his ownership of the social media platform that remains the most powerful communication channel in the Us. Having accepted his shilling and let him into the gang, Trump may find Musk-who knows how to bear a grudge just as much as he doesdifficult to shrug off. While it may be fine to drop in on the president-elect at Mar-a-Lago,once he's Leader ofthe Free World,this becomes a lot harder.
